threats.scenario: GET /rest/admin/application-configuration returns full config object without authorization (server.ts:605). Exposes Google OAuth client IDs, application domain, security-relevant settings, and server base URL. GET /rest/admin/application-version also unauthenticated, revealing exact version for targeted CVE attacks.

remediation.title: Add authorization to admin endpoints; return only necessary configuration data
steps: Add security.isAuthorized() then role check for admin on these endpoints
Limit configuration response to non-sensitive frontend config only
Move sensitive config to server-side only

threats.scenario: API Users endpoint POST /api/Users allows mass assignment — user can set role='admin' during registration since Sequelize processes all provided fields via the finale-rest auto-generated endpoint. The model validates role values but request body fields are not allowlisted.
threats.controls_in_place: Role validation in Sequelize model (allowlist of valid values), but no protection against mass assignment

remediation.title: Implement allowlist-based field filtering for user registration
steps: In the User creation endpoint, explicitly pick allowed fields: { email, password, username } only
Never expose role/admin fields via the public registration API
Add explicit exclusion of role from user creation

threats.scenario: Admin actions (user management, product changes, order status changes) are not specifically audited. Morgan access logging captures HTTP requests but does not capture who performed admin actions or what changed. Admin activity cannot be reconstructed from logs.

remediation.title: Add structured audit logging for admin operations
steps: Create an audit log middleware for admin routes that captures user ID, action, resource, and change details
Store audit log separately from access log

threats.scenario: GET /support/logs serves an index of application log files (server.ts:267) with serve-index. Log files accessible via GET /support/logs/:file. Access logs (Morgan combined format) capture Authorization headers, user agents, and full request URIs including query parameters (e.g. password in URL).

remediation.title: Remove public log directory browsing; require admin auth for log access
steps: Remove or protect /support/logs route with security.isAuthorized() + admin role check
Configure Morgan to exclude Authorization headers from logs
Prevent logging of sensitive query parameters
